story_title: Israeli buffer zone in southern Lebanon

corpus.title: Israeli attacks on medics in southern Lebanon part of 'alarming pattern,' says MSF
corpus.signal_text:
Israeli attacks on medics in southern Lebanon part of 'alarming pattern,' says MSF

International organization Doctors Without Borders says a Tuesday drone strike that killed two paramedics and injured another in southern Lebanon is part of an "alarming pattern" of Israeli attacks targeting first responders.

corpus.title: Sport helping displaced Lebanese living in stadium to cope
corpus.signal_text:
Sport helping displaced Lebanese living in stadium to cope

More than a million people have been forced to flee their homes in southern Lebanon due to fighting between Israeli forces and Hezbollah. As many shelter in Beirut stadiums, sport is sometimes a welcome distraction.

corpus.title: Hezbollah chief hails Iran support for resistance, vows not to surrender to Israel
corpus.signal_text:
Hezbollah chief hails Iran support for resistance, vows not to surrender to Israel

Tehran, IRNA – Hezbollah Secretary-General Sheikh Naim Qassem reiterates the firmness of the Lebanese resistance movement against the Israeli aggression, saying it will neither surrender to the occupying regime nor allow it to realize its objectives in the Arab country.

corpus.title: Staff Sergeant Negev Dagan was killed by mortar fire in southern Lebanon
corpus.signal_text:
Staff Sergeant Negev Dagan Was Killed by Mortar Fire in Southern Lebanon
Dagan, a Golani Brigade combat soldier, was killed in the Litani River area. His death brings the number of Israeli soldiers killed since the start of the current round of fighting with Lebanon to 19
Staff Sgt. Negev Dagan, 20, from southern Israel, was killed Thursday by a mortar shell in the Litani River area of southern Lebanon during cross-border fighting with Hezbollah, the Israeli military said early on Friday, bringing the number of Israeli soldiers killed since the current round of hostilities began to 19.
The Eshkol Regional Council, where Dagan lived, said in a eulogy: "We are shocked and saddened by the loss of our dear council member. Negev was the son of Hava and Shalom and one of six siblings. Negev was a sociable, opinionated young man who was popular among his friends. Even as a teenager, he worked diligently, loved fixing old cars, and was interested in hands-on work and forging his own path. We share in the deep sorrow of his family, friends, and the community of Dekel."
Since the cease-fire in Lebanon began last month, soldiers and civilians have continued to be injured by Hezbollah fire, most of them in explosive drone attacks.
On Thursday, an explosive-laden drone from Lebanon hit the parking lot of Rosh Hanikra National Park, without warning sirens being activated. Three civilians were wounded – one in critical condition from shrapnel and blast injuries, one moderately injured, and one lightly injured, the Galilee Medical Center in Nahariya said. The IDF said the drone was launched by Hezbollah and that it constitutes a "blatant violation of the ceasefire understandings" in Lebanon.
Earlier this week, Master Sgt. (res.) Alexander Glovanyov, 47, from Petah Tikva, was killed by an explosive drone strike in Israeli territory near the Lebanese border. His mother, Flora, said that he was killed while at the military base.
The IDF said late last month that drones operating over the combat zone in southern Lebanon had recently become one of the military's biggest operational challenges. Military officials said the army believes countering Hezbollah's drone network requires targeting the group's broader infrastructure, but added that restrictions imposed by Israel's political leadership are currently preventing a wider campaign against the threat.

corpus.title: IDF would withdraw from Lebanon if Hezbollah disarmed as CIA plans to remove terror weapons - KAN
corpus.signal_text:
Israel and Lebanon are expected to agree to an IDF withdrawal from Lebanese territory in exchange for Hezbollah's disarmament, Israel's public broadcaster KAN News reported on Friday.
The report added that the CIA prepared a plan to disarm the Iran-backed terror group.
Delegations from Israel and Lebanon are expected to meet on Friday for continued US-facilitated peace talks.
The talks on Thursday were “productive and positive,” according to a State Department official. “We look forward to continuing this tomorrow and hope to have more to share then," the official added.
IDF strikes southern Lebanon after peace talks on Thursday
This comes as the IDF carries out strikes in southern Lebanon, Army Radio reported on Friday.
Additionally, the IDF struck a rocket launcher in southern Lebanon that fired projectiles into the northern Israeli city of Kiryat Shmona during the overnight between Thursday and Friday. The military noted that it struck several pieces of terror infrastructure overnight as well.

corpus.title: Lebanon seeks truce extension as Israeli strikes continue
corpus.signal_text:
Breadcrumb
Lebanon seeks truce extension as Israeli strikes, evacuation orders continue
Lebanon and Israel entered a third round of talks in Washington on Friday amid continued Israeli strikes on southern and eastern Lebanon, fresh forced evacuation orders, and the killing of an Israeli soldier during clashes with Hezbollah.
Official Lebanese sources told The New Arab that Beirut is seeking a renewed extension of the ceasefire, alongside guarantees that any truce would be "complete and final" rather than "fragile and repeatedly violated" as it has been since coming into force on 17 April.
The talks, which mark the third round of negotiations between Beirut and Tel Aviv but with a higher level of representation, come as the US pushes for a broader security arrangement between the two sides.
The Lebanese side stressed during the Washington discussions "the necessity of a comprehensive and complete ceasefire" and demanded an end to Israeli bulldozing and destruction operations in southern Lebanon.
They also called for a full Israeli withdrawal, the release of prisoners, and the return of displaced civilians.
Israel, meanwhile, has maintained that its operations are aimed at removing threats against northern settlements and that it reserves the right to take military action against any "imminent or planned" attacks. Israeli officials have also insisted they will not fully withdraw while Hezbollah remains armed.
Lebanese Parliament Speaker Nabih Berri warned that the success of the negotiations depended on a genuine halt to hostilities.
"The negotiations begin today at nine o’clock Washington time, and if a real ceasefire is not achieved, that means everything has collapsed," Berri told Lebanese newspaper Ad-Diyar.
Berri added that Lebanon would accept "nothing less than the withdrawal of the Israeli occupation army, followed by reconstruction, the deployment of the Lebanese army, and the return of residents".
A US State Department official told The New Arab that direct communication between Lebanon and Israel represented the "best way" to quickly reach a lasting peace and security agreement, while accusing Hezbollah of attempting to obstruct negotiations through attacks and threats.
The diplomatic push unfolded alongside renewed violence on the ground.
The Israeli army announced on Friday that Sergeant First Class Negev Dagan, 20, had been killed "during the fighting in southern Lebanon", bringing the number of Israeli military personnel and contractors killed

corpus.title: Israel issues forced displacement orders for five towns and villages in southern Lebanon
corpus.signal_text:
Israel issues forced displacement orders for five towns and villages in southern Lebanon

Israel issues forced displacement orders for five towns and villages in southern Lebanon Israel’s military has issued a forced evacuation order for the residents of five towns and villages amid its ongoing war on Lebanon. In a post on social media, the military listed the targeted areas in the south of the country as Shabriha, Hamideh, Zuqaq al-Mufdi, Mashouk and El Haouch. Despite continuing Israeli attacks and occupation, many people in the south still believe the armed group is the only force capable of defending them.

Eight children are among those reported dead after a series of air strikes hit the south of Beirut.

corpus.title: Inside the Christian border town in Lebanon that says it survived by defying Hezbollah

Inside the Christian border town in Lebanon that says it survived by defying Hezbollah

Residents of Rmeish, a Christian town in southern Lebanon, say they confronted Hezbollah and blocked them from using the village area.

corpus.title: In Lebanon, the idea of peace with Israel continues to deeply divide opinion
corpus.signal_text:
In Lebanon, the idea of peace with Israel continues to deeply divide opinion

In Lebanon, the very idea of peace with Israel continues to deeply divide opinion. As unprecedented negotiations open today in Washington under US mediation, many Lebanese see a chance for a return to stability. But within Hezbollah’s camp, any normalisation with Israel is firmly rejected. FRANCE 24's Serge Berberi and Renee Davis report.
